name: Architecture College Platform
description:
Merged metadata descriptions from all Architecture College Platform microservices.

# account-service
Handles signup for students, teachers, and admins, JWT login, and authenticated profile access.

# curriculum-service
Academic catalog: careers, areas, professional lines, obligatory and elective subjects, and subject-series relationships.

# enrollment-service
Group and student enrollments, enrollment lock rules, and Mongo-backed bulk preload jobs alongside Postgres transactions.

# grade-service
Captures grades, validation/authorization flows, teacher grading consoles, and academic history projections.

# schedule-service
Class groups (obligatory/elective): create, query, attach teachers, adjust capacity, and emit scheduling-related integration events over RabbitMQ.

# student-service
Student master data: CRUD, lookups, professional line assignment, semester progression, and RabbitMQ integration for domain events.

# teacher-service
Faculty lifecycle: create and delete teachers, validate account numbers, batch lookups, and RabbitMQ integration. JWT validation aligned with account-service.

problemStatement.problemDescription:
Combined problem descriptions from all Architecture College Platform microservices.

# account-service
Academic platforms need distinct onboarding paths per persona while sharing a single identity model.

# curriculum-service
Curriculum data is hierarchical (careers, areas, subjects) and must stay normalized, version-friendly, and easy for enrollment and scheduling to consume.

# enrollment-service
Enrollment windows mix strong consistency for seat assignment with heavy bulk preload and status tracking that should not block the OLTP path.

# grade-service
Grades must be auditable and authorizable, while students and teachers need fast, role-appropriate views of history and pending work.

# schedule-service
Timetabling ties classrooms, teachers, and enrollment capacity; updates must stay consistent and visible to enrollment and faculty workflows.

# student-service
Student profiles and progression (line, semester) must stay consistent while peer services react without tight synchronous coupling.

# teacher-service
Scheduling and grading need authoritative faculty records with safeguards against duplicate payroll identifiers and clear integration with identity tokens.
problemList: Academic platforms need distinct onboarding paths per persona while sharing a single identity model.
Curriculum data is hierarchical (careers, areas, subjects) and must stay normalized, version-friendly, and easy for enrollment and scheduling to consume.
Enrollment windows mix strong consistency for seat assignment with heavy bulk preload and status tracking that should not block the OLTP path.
Grades must be auditable and authorizable, while students and teachers need fast, role-appropriate views of history and pending work.
Timetabling ties classrooms, teachers, and enrollment capacity; updates must stay consistent and visible to enrollment and faculty workflows.
Student profiles and progression (line, semester) must stay consistent while peer services react without tight synchronous coupling.
Scheduling and grading need authoritative faculty records with safeguards against duplicate payroll identifiers and clear integration with identity tokens.

solutionList.title: Segmented signup
solutionList.description: Dedicated endpoints for student, teacher, and admin registration with role-aware payloads.

solutionList.title: Centralized login
solutionList.description: Single /login path issuing JWT tokens consumed by downstream services.

solutionList.title: Profile surface
solutionList.description: /my-profile for authenticated users without leaking cross-tenant data.

solutionList.title: Bounded REST resources
solutionList.description: Controllers map to aggregates: careers, areas, professional lines, obligatory/elective subjects, subject series.

solutionList.title: Single relational catalog
solutionList.description: Postgres curriculum_db is the source of truth; no shared tables with other services.

solutionList.title: Rich read shapes
solutionList.description: Queries by area, career, semester, and series support admin tooling and downstream services.

solutionList.title: Polyglot persistence
solutionList.description: PostgreSQL holds authoritative enrollment rows; MongoDB stores preload process state and auxiliary documents.

solutionList.title: Dedicated REST surfaces
solutionList.description: Command vs finder controllers separate writes from reads; student self-service has its own routes.

solutionList.title: Bulk preload APIs
solutionList.description: Students, subjects, schedules, and grades each expose preload + status + clear lifecycles for operators.

solutionList.title: Split controllers by audience
solutionList.description: Admin-grade queries, teacher group grading, and student self-service reads each have focused routes.

solutionList.title: Postgres + Mongo
solutionList.description: Transactional grade rows in PostgreSQL; Mongo supports projections and document-oriented reads where configured.

solutionList.title: RabbitMQ
solutionList.description: Async hooks (e.g. academic history refresh) decouple heavy updates from the request path.

solutionList.title: Dedicated REST controllers
solutionList.description: Creation, finder, update, and deletion flows are split for clarity and evolution.

solutionList.title: Capacity and roster operations
solutionList.description: Endpoints adjust spots and assign or remove teachers with explicit paths.

solutionList.title: Event-friendly runtime
solutionList.description: RabbitMQ supports notifying peers when groups or schedules change.

solutionList.title: Command vs query split
solutionList.description: StudentCommandController handles writes; StudentQueryController optimizes reads.

solutionList.title: Relational source of truth
solutionList.description: student_db stores canonical student aggregates under this service boundary.

solutionList.title: AMQP integration
solutionList.description: RabbitMQ propagates events for downstream sync (as implemented in the module).

solutionList.title: Explicit validation endpoint
solutionList.description: Pre-validate teacher account numbers before expensive downstream flows.

solutionList.title: Command vs query APIs
solutionList.description: Writes under /v1/api/teachers; reads under /teachers (legacy path — normalize at gateway).

solutionList.title: Broker-backed integration
solutionList.description: RabbitMQ keeps schedule/grade consumers loosely coupled (as implemented).

designPatterns.title: Database per service
designPatterns.description: Account data lives only in account_db; no shared user tables with other services.

designPatterns.title: Token-based API security
designPatterns.description: Issue JWTs at login; other services validate the same signing key.

designPatterns.title: Database per service
designPatterns.description: Only curriculum_db holds catalog tables; no cross-schema joins with enrollment or schedule.

designPatterns.title: Aggregate-oriented APIs
designPatterns.description: One controller family per aggregate reduces coupling and keeps URLs predictable.

designPatterns.title: Database per service
designPatterns.description: enrollment_db schema is owned here; no foreign keys to other services' tables.

designPatterns.title: Polyglot persistence
designPatterns.description: OLTP in Postgres; Mongo for flexible preload documents and progress tracking.

designPatterns.title: Database per service
designPatterns.description: grade_db (SQL + document URI) owned by this service; no cross-service table sharing.

designPatterns.title: CQSP-style splits
designPatterns.description: Separate query and command controllers for grades reduce accidental coupling on writes.

designPatterns.title: Command/query split
designPatterns.description: /v1/api/groups vs /v1/api/finder/groups separates writes from read-optimized queries.

designPatterns.title: Database per service
designPatterns.description: student_db is owned only by this service.

designPatterns.title: CQRS-style HTTP split
designPatterns.description: Separate controllers for commands and queries.

designPatterns.title: Database per service
designPatterns.description: teacher_db owned solely by this service.

designPatterns.title: Strangler path normalization
designPatterns.description: Gateway maps legacy /teachers reads to versioned public paths.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless instances
scalabilityStrategies.description: Scale out multiple Account Service replicas behind a load balancer; no server session affinity.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Dedicated database
scalabilityStrategies.description: Tune account_db independently from curriculum or enrollment workloads.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless horizontal scale
scalabilityStrategies.description: Read-heavy catalog scales with replica count and DB read tuning.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Optional read caching later
scalabilityStrategies.description: CDN or Redis for hot GET paths (e.g. full career trees) if metrics justify it.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Scale stateless API tier
scalabilityStrategies.description: Horizontal replicas with sticky-less load balancing.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Isolate bulk preload load
scalabilityStrategies.description: Throttle or queue heavy preloads so OLTP enrollment latency stays predictable.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless HTTP tier
scalabilityStrategies.description: Scale Boot instances; JWT carries identity; no server session affinity required.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Tune brokers and DB pools
scalabilityStrategies.description: Rabbit consumers and JDBC pool sizes scale with traffic separately.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API tier
scalabilityStrategies.description: Scale replicas; offload heavy reporting with read replicas or cached projections if needed.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Broker decoupling
scalabilityStrategies.description: RabbitMQ absorbs peak fan-out to peers without synchronous chains.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Tune broker consumers
scalabilityStrategies.description: Prefetch and listener concurrency for peak registration periods.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Stateless API replicas
scalabilityStrategies.description: Scale JVMs; no session stickiness required.

scalabilityStrategies.title: Rabbit consumer tuning
scalabilityStrategies.description: Scale listeners or partition workloads by routing key if needed.

securityStrategies.title: JWT propagation
securityStrategies.description: This service mints tokens; consumers must validate signature, expiry, and roles.

securityStrategies.title: Secrets not in Git
securityStrategies.description: JWT secret and DB passwords come from environment / secret manager in real deployment.

securityStrategies.title: Gateway + service auth
securityStrategies.description: Protect mutating routes in production; align with institutional RBAC.

securityStrategies.title: Least-privilege DB role
securityStrategies.description: DB user limited to curriculum_db only.

securityStrategies.title: Student vs admin routes
securityStrategies.description: Different route families for self-service vs operator preload; enforce with Spring Security + gateway.

securityStrategies.title: Secrets for both datastores
securityStrategies.description: RDS/Atlas credentials via environment or secret manager — never committed.

securityStrategies.title: JWT validation
securityStrategies.description: Resource server validates tokens; teacher/student routes derive account from the JWT.

securityStrategies.title: Role-aware mutations
securityStrategies.description: Restrict group mutations to operators; student-facing traffic should not hit dangerous routes.

securityStrategies.title: Authenticated mutations
securityStrategies.description: Align write routes with Spring Security and gateway policies.

securityStrategies.title: PII handling
securityStrategies.description: Student data — minimize logging; encrypt at rest where required.

securityStrategies.title: Shared JWT signing key
securityStrategies.description: Must match account-service secret; never commit production values.

cacheStrategies.name: No default application cache
cacheStrategies.description: Optional Redis if token blocklists or rate limiting are added later.

cacheStrategies.name: Not enabled by default
cacheStrategies.description: Add application or edge cache when profiling shows catalog hot spots.

cacheStrategies.name: None by default
cacheStrategies.description: Consider Redis for idempotency keys or rate limits on enrollment spikes.

cacheStrategies.name: None by default
cacheStrategies.description: Add Redis or CDN if read-heavy catalog or history endpoints need it.

cacheStrategies.name: Optional catalog cache
cacheStrategies.description: Cache hot finder responses (e.g. current term slice) if metrics justify it.

cacheStrategies.name: Optional read cache
cacheStrategies.description: Cache filter-heavy GET /by responses if profiling demands it.

cacheStrategies.name: None default
cacheStrategies.description: Optional cache for validate-account hot path.

requestFlow.title: REST call
requestFlow.description: Clients hit /v1/api/students for reads or writes.

requestFlow.title: Business rules
requestFlow.description: Validation, professional line rules, semester increments.

requestFlow.title: Persist and notify

requestFlow.title: Authenticate
requestFlow.description: JWT validated for protected routes.

requestFlow.title: Invoke teacher API

requestFlow.title: Persist / integrate

eventFlow.title: No domain events in v1
eventFlow.description: Account Service focuses on synchronous HTTP; optional outbox can be added later.

eventFlow.title: Synchronous integration
eventFlow.description: This service does not publish AMQP by default; peers integrate via REST.

eventFlow.title: Future eventing
eventFlow.description: Optional domain events (e.g. catalog change) can be added with an outbox pattern.

eventFlow.title: Preload lifecycle
eventFlow.description: POST /preload starts work; GET /preload/{id}/status polls Mongo-backed state.

eventFlow.title: Downstream visibility
eventFlow.description: Schedule/grade services read enrollment facts via HTTP or shared events (if added later).

eventFlow.title: Domain signal
eventFlow.description: Grade or history changes may publish or consume AMQP messages.

eventFlow.title: Projection refresh
eventFlow.description: Listeners update academic history or related aggregates.

eventFlow.title: Publish
eventFlow.description: AMQP messages emitted on schedule/group lifecycle events (as wired in code).

eventFlow.title: Consume
eventFlow.description: Peer services update projections or trigger workflows.

eventFlow.title: Emit
eventFlow.description: Student lifecycle events on Rabbit (as wired).

eventFlow.title: Consume
eventFlow.description: Peers refresh projections or trigger workflows.

eventFlow.title: Teacher lifecycle signal
eventFlow.description: Messages on create/delete or other events (per wiring).

decisions.title: Spring Cloud Netflix (Eureka + Config)
decisions.problem: Multiple JVM services need discovery and consistent configuration.
decisions.solution: Use Eureka registration and Spring Cloud Config with config-data YAML.
decisions.outcome: Operational parity between local Compose and target deployment.
alternatives: Consul for discovery and KV config

decisions.title: Relational model for curriculum graph
decisions.problem: Careers, areas, and subjects need constraints and joins for integrity.
decisions.solution: PostgreSQL + JPA entities with explicit relationships.
decisions.outcome: Enrollment and scheduling can trust foreign keys and uniqueness in curriculum_db.
alternatives: Document store for entire catalog tree

decisions.title: Postgres + Mongo in one service
decisions.problem: Enrollments need ACID; bulk preload status needs flexible schema.
decisions.solution: JPA for enrollments; Mongo templates/repositories for process documents.
decisions.outcome: Operators get progress APIs without bloating relational tables.
alternatives: Single Postgres JSONB column for job state

decisions.title: Dual store
decisions.problem: Some reads suit documents while grades stay relational.
decisions.solution: PostgreSQL for grades; Mongo URI configured for grade_db projections.
decisions.outcome: Flexibility without giving up transactional grade rows.
alternatives: Postgres-only JSON columns for all projections
Event sourcing with separate read stack

decisions.title: Netflix Eureka + Config
decisions.problem: Platform already standardizes on Spring Cloud Netflix.
decisions.solution: Reuse Eureka and Config Server for discovery and YAML.

decisions.title: Separate finder base path
decisions.problem: Read-heavy schedule queries should not compete with transactional endpoints.
decisions.outcome: Clearer caching and scaling story for reads.

decisions.title: Command/query controllers
decisions.problem: Write models differ from read traffic patterns.
decisions.outcome: Clearer evolution of validation and query optimization.

decisions.title: Split command and query base paths
decisions.problem: Legacy read API used /teachers without version prefix.
decisions.outcome: Backward compatibility internally; gateway should unify externally.
alternatives: Single controller with explicit versioning headers
